multipleTestingNote: 3 pre-registered event(s) x 7 horizons = 21 cells screened. P-values are NOT corrected for multiple testing; treat a single green cell among many as weak evidence.

events.name: GLD oversold washout below its 200-day SMA
events.rationale: A mean-reversion proxy, NOT a replication of the SentimenTrader GLD study (which rests on a proprietary Optix sentiment index and a 21-year seasonal window we cannot reproduce). Mechanism: a liquid, widely-held commodity ETF that becomes acutely oversold (RSI below 30) while already trading below its 200-day SMA has usually overshot on momentum or forced selling, so a partial snap-back toward the mean is common over the following weeks. Deliberately a COUNTER-TREND test, logged as such, not a trend signal. Forward returns on GLD; significance from our random-entry Monte Carlo; thin-sample caveat applies.
events.definition: Trigger on the session GLD's RSI(14) crosses below 30 while GLD closes below its 200-day SMA. Forward returns measured on GLD from the trigger close.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: SLV oversold washout below its 200-day SMA
events.rationale: The same oversold-in-downtrend mean-reversion construct as the GLD card, applied to silver to test whether the washout-bounce holds across precious metals. Silver (SLV) is higher-beta and more volatile than gold, so washouts run deeper and snap-backs are larger but noisier; the natural robustness check on the gold result. Counter-trend by design. Forward returns on SLV; significance from our random-entry Monte Carlo; thin-sample caveat applies.
events.definition: Trigger on the session SLV's RSI(14) crosses below 30 while SLV closes below its 200-day SMA. Forward returns measured on SLV from the trigger close.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: S&P 500 strong 2nd quarter → 9-month forward
events.rationale: A strong second quarter — a price gain in each of April, May and June, and/or a quarterly price gain of 10 per cent or more from the end of March to the end of June — is a momentum-and-participation regime signal. Broad, sustained strength into mid-year has historically been followed by above-base-rate S&P 500 price returns over the following nine months (June close to the following March close). This is not a replication of the SentimenTrader record, which is a proprietary lead re-tested here on our own data; the card reports OUR numbers only. The honest edge is the excess over the unconditional nine-month base rate — the S&P 500 is already positive over roughly four in five nine-month windows — not the raw win rate, and signals cluster in secular bull markets, so the effective sample is thinner than the raw count. A long-only seasonal context signal, judged on our random-entry Monte Carlo, with a thin-sample caveat carried on the card.
events.definition: At each June month-end, flag a 'strong' 2nd quarter when the S&P 500 posts a price gain in each of April, May and June, and/or gains 10% or more from the end of March to the end of June. Forward returns measured on the S&P 500 price index from the June close over 3/6/9/12 months (9M is the headline: June close to the following March close).
events.entryNote: Forward return measured on the monthly price index from the June month-end close (event-study convention).
